---
title: Runtime State
description: Runtime tracking for incremental recalculation and dirty propagation
---

## Overview

Runtime state structures track version numbers and dirty flags for incremental computation. Each span maintains version counters that trigger cache invalidation when mismatched, and dirty queues batch work across recalc pipelines.

## DirtyBits

Bit flags indicating which recalc pipelines need to process a span.

### Values

<ResponseField name="kNone" type="0">
  No recalculation needed. Span is fully up-to-date.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="kTopology" type="1 << 0">
  Topology changed (connectivity, endpoints). Requires re-resolving span endpoints and updating connection indices.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="kGeometry" type="1 << 1">
  Geometry needs rebuild (curve tessellation). Triggered by endpoint moves or `GeometrySettings` changes.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="kBounds" type="1 << 2">
  Spatial bounds need rebuild. Always follows geometry changes to maintain consistency.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="kRender" type="1 << 3">
  Visual cache needs rebuild (support structures, insulators). Triggered by port position changes or `VisualSettings` updates.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="kRaycast" type="1 << 4">
  Raycast acceleration structures need rebuild. Used for selection and spatial queries.
</ResponseField>

### Operators

DirtyBits supports bitwise operations:

```cpp
// Combine flags
DirtyBits dirty = DirtyBits::kGeometry | DirtyBits::kBounds;

// Check if any flag is set
if (any(dirty_bits, DirtyBits::kGeometry)) {
  rebuild_geometry();
}

// Mask off flags
dirty_bits &= ~DirtyBits::kGeometry; // Clear geometry bit
```

## SpanRuntimeState

Tracks runtime version numbers and dirty flags for a single span.

### Fields

<ResponseField name="span_id" type="ObjectId" default="kInvalidObjectId">
  The span this runtime state tracks. Used as a consistency check.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="data_version" type="std::uint64_t" default="0">
  Incremented whenever span authoritative data changes (endpoints, properties). Used to detect when caches need invalidation.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="geometry_version" type="std::uint64_t" default="0">
  Version number when geometry (curve tessellation) was last computed. Stored in `CurveCacheEntry::source_version` for mismatch detection.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="bounds_version" type="std::uint64_t" default="0">
  Version number when spatial bounds were last computed. Stored in `BoundsCacheEntry::source_version`.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="render_version" type="std::uint64_t" default="0">
  Version number when visual details were last computed. Stored in `SpanVisualCacheEntry::source_version`.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="raycast_version" type="std::uint64_t" default="0">
  Version number when raycast structures were last built. Used for selection/picking invalidation.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="dirty_bits" type="DirtyBits" default="kNone">
  Bit flags indicating which recalc pipelines need to process this span. Cleared as each pipeline completes.
</ResponseField>

## Version Tracking Flow

When a span is modified:

1. **Edit Operation** - User moves a pole, changing connected port positions
2. **Bump Data Version** - `data_version++` for affected spans
3. **Mark Dirty** - Set `DirtyBits::kGeometry | DirtyBits::kBounds | ...`
4. **Queue for Recalc** - Add `span_id` to `DirtyQueue::geometry_dirty_span_ids`
5. **Commit** - Process dirty queues in dependency order
6. **Rebuild Geometry** - Tessellate curve, store result in `CurveCache`
7. **Update Version** - Copy `data_version` to `geometry_version`, store in `CurveCacheEntry::source_version`
8. **Clear Dirty Bit** - Remove `DirtyBits::kGeometry` flag

If another edit occurs before commit, step 2 increments `data_version` again, ensuring the next commit detects the mismatch.

## DirtyQueue

Batches span IDs needing recalculation, organized by pipeline stage.

### Fields

<ResponseField name="topology_dirty_span_ids" type="std::vector<ObjectId>" required>
  Spans needing topology updates (endpoint resolution, index rebuilds).
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="geometry_dirty_span_ids" type="std::vector<ObjectId>" required>
  Spans needing geometry rebuild (curve tessellation). Processed after topology.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="bounds_dirty_span_ids" type="std::vector<ObjectId>" required>
  Spans needing bounds rebuild. Processed after geometry to use latest curve points.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="render_dirty_span_ids" type="std::vector<ObjectId>" required>
  Spans needing visual cache rebuild (support structures). Can run in parallel with bounds.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="raycast_dirty_span_ids" type="std::vector<ObjectId>" required>
  Spans needing raycast structure rebuild. Typically deferred until user interaction.
</ResponseField>

## Processing Order

Dirty queues are processed in dependency order during `CoreState::Commit()`:

1. **Topology** - Resolve endpoints, update connection indices
2. **Geometry** - Tessellate curves (depends on topology)
3. **Bounds** - Compute AABBs (depends on geometry)
4. **Render** - Generate visual details (depends on topology)
5. **Raycast** - Build acceleration structures (depends on geometry/bounds)

This ensures derived caches never reference stale data.

## Querying Runtime State

Use `CoreView::find_span_runtime_state()` to inspect version numbers:

```cpp
const SpanRuntimeState* state = core_view.find_span_runtime_state(span_id);
if (state) {
  // Check if any work is pending
  if (state->dirty_bits != DirtyBits::kNone) {
    std::cout << "Span has pending recalc\n";
  }
  
  // Inspect version numbers
  std::cout << "Data version: " << state->data_version << "\n";
  std::cout << "Geometry version: " << state->geometry_version << "\n";
  
  // Detect stale geometry cache
  const CurveCacheEntry* curve = core_state.find_curve_cache(span_id);
  if (curve && curve->source_version != state->geometry_version) {
    std::cout << "Geometry cache is stale!\n";
  }
}
```

## Use Cases

### Incremental Updates
```cpp
// Edit multiple spans
auto result1 = core_state.MovePole(pole_id_1, new_transform);
auto result2 = core_state.MovePole(pole_id_2, new_transform);

// Batch commit recalculates all affected spans
CommitResult commit = core_state.Commit();
std::cout << "Processed " << commit.recalc_stats.geometry_processed << " spans\n";
```

### Lazy Evaluation
```cpp
CommitOptions options;
options.run_recalc = false; // Skip recalc, just accumulate dirty flags

for (ObjectId pole_id : large_batch) {
  core_state.MovePole(pole_id, transforms[pole_id]);
}

// Commit once at the end
options.run_recalc = true;
core_state.Commit(options);
```

### Debugging Dirty State
```cpp
void print_dirty_queue_stats(const DirtyQueue& queue) {
  std::cout << "Topology: " << queue.topology_dirty_span_ids.size() << "\n";
  std::cout << "Geometry: " << queue.geometry_dirty_span_ids.size() << "\n";
  std::cout << "Bounds: " << queue.bounds_dirty_span_ids.size() << "\n";
  std::cout << "Render: " << queue.render_dirty_span_ids.size() << "\n";
  std::cout << "Raycast: " << queue.raycast_dirty_span_ids.size() << "\n";
}
```

- [BoundsCache](/api/cache/bounds-cache) - Uses `bounds_version` for invalidation
- [VisualCache](/api/cache/visual-cache) - Uses `render_version` for invalidation
